comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Align unset-value wording across the ticket (`<unset>` vs `null`).**

The new rationale says secrets render as `null` when unset, but the Outcome section still documents `"<unset>"`. Please make both sections match one documented behavior.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested wording update</summary>

```diff
-- `codogotchi config list` prints the full config as formatted JSON (with credentials redacted: PAT and Wakatime key shown as `<set>` or `<unset>`).
+- `codogotchi config list` prints the full config as formatted JSON (with credentials redacted: PAT and Wakatime key shown as `<set>` or `null`).
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@docs/product/delivery/phase-01/ticket-16-cli-config.md` around lines 59 - 60,
The documentation currently contradicts itself about how unset credentials
display (the Rationale says they render as null but the Outcome lists
"<unset>"); pick one canonical representation and update both occurrences so
they match—specifically update the "Credential redaction in `list`" sentence and
the "`config get` output" lines so they both use the same token (`null` or
`"<unset>"`) consistently throughout the ticket text.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:triton:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Remove duplicated rationale block to keep one source of truth.**

This section repeats the same implementation notes twice with slightly different wording, which increases maintenance drift risk.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested cleanup</summary>

```diff
 - **Schema lives in `packages/contracts/src/config.ts`** as `codogotchiConfigSchema` (zod) plus `SETTABLE_TOP_LEVEL`/`SETTABLE_HEALTH_KEYS` allow-lists. `resolveConfigPath` is the authoritative dotted-path resolver — anything it returns `null` for is unknown or intentionally read-only.
 - **`profile_id` is read-only.** `config get profile_id` works; `config set profile_id …` is refused. Rotating it would orphan the server-side profile.
 - **Atomic writes.** Reuses `writeConfig` (tmp + rename). Kill mid-write leaves either previous or new content; never partial.
 - **Type validation:** bool (exactly `true`/`false`), non-negative finite number, ISO date or `"null"`, non-empty string, https URL with trailing slash strip, nullable secrets via the `"null"` literal.
 - **`list` redaction** renders `github_token` and `wakatime_key` as `"<set>"` when populated, `null` when not. No secret bytes appear in output.
 - **`config get` output:** strings bare, non-strings as JSON.
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
- **Schema lives in `packages/contracts/src/config.ts`** as `codogotchiConfigSchema` (zod) plus an allow-list of `SETTABLE_TOP_LEVEL` and `SETTABLE_HEALTH_KEYS` keys. `resolveConfigPath` is the authoritative dotted-path resolver — anything it returns `null` for is unknown or intentionally read-only (e.g. `profile_id`).
- **`profile_id` is read-only.** `config get profile_id` works (so users can copy it for support); `config set profile_id …` is refused with `Unknown or read-only config key`. Rotating `profile_id` would orphan the server-side profile, so this is deliberate.
- **Atomic writes.** `configSet` uses the existing `writeConfig` from P1.12, which writes to `${target}.tmp-<pid>-<ts>` and `rename`s. A kill mid-write leaves either the previous good content or the new good content — never a partial JSON document.
- **Type validation per field.**
  - Booleans (`health.weekend_decay`): accepts exactly `"true"` / `"false"`; anything else rejected.
  - Numbers (`health.grace_days`, `health.decay_per_day`, `health.revive_threshold`, `health.revive_hp`): finite non-negative; rejects `nope` and `-5`.
  - Nullable date (`health.vacation_until`): accepts ISO date string or the literal `"null"`/`""` to clear; normalizes to `Date.toISOString()`.
  - Required string (`health.timezone`, `handle`): non-empty.
  - URL (`convex_http_url`): must be `https://`; trailing slashes stripped.
  - Nullable secrets (`github_token`, `github_username`, `wakatime_key`): the literal `"null"` clears the field; otherwise stored verbatim.
- **Credential redaction in `list`.** PAT and Wakatime key render as `"<set>"` when present and `null` when unset. No PAT/Wakatime bytes appear in output. Other config fields are passed through as-is.
- **`config get` output.** Strings print bare; non-string values print as JSON (so `health.grace_days` → `2`, `health.weekend_decay` → `false`, `health` (whole object) → JSON).
- **Subagent review.** Code ticket; subagent runs.
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@docs/product/delivery/phase-01/ticket-16-cli-config.md` around lines 42 - 61,
The file contains a duplicated implementation/rationale block describing config
behavior; remove the redundant block so there is a single authoritative
description (keep the more complete version that references
codogotchiConfigSchema, SETTABLE_TOP_LEVEL/SETTABLE_HEALTH_KEYS,
resolveConfigPath, configSet and writeConfig). Edit out the earlier/shorter
duplicate paragraph and ensure the retained section includes the read-only note
for profile_id, atomic write details (writeConfig/tmp+rename), per-field
validation rules, and credential redaction/list/get behavior so all referenced
symbols (codogotchiConfigSchema, resolveConfigPath, SETTABLE_TOP_LEVEL,
configSet, writeConfig) remain documented once.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:triton:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Normalize missing optional config values in `configGet` output.**

`github_username` is an allowed key but may be absent in older configs; on Line 180–182 this can produce `undefined\n` for a valid key. Normalize `undefined` to `null` (or another explicit sentinel) before formatting to keep CLI output stable.

 

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 export async function configGet(opts: ConfigGetOptions): Promise<string> {
 	const config = await loadOrFail(opts.home);
-	const value = getDottedValue(config, opts.path);
-	return typeof value === "string"
-		? `${value}\n`
-		: `${JSON.stringify(value)}\n`;
+	const value = getDottedValue(config, opts.path);
+	const normalized = value === undefined ? null : value;
+	return typeof normalized === "string"
+		? `${normalized}\n`
+		: `${JSON.stringify(normalized)}\n`;
 }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@packages/cli/src/config-command.ts` around lines 177 - 183, The configGet
function returns "undefined\n" for valid but missing optional keys; update
configGet (which calls getDottedValue) to normalize undefined to a stable
sentinel (e.g., null) before formatting: if the retrieved value is undefined set
it to null, then continue with the existing string vs JSON formatting logic so
callers get "null\n" (or your chosen sentinel) instead of "undefined\n".
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- walkthrough_start -->

## Walkthrough

This pull request adds a complete CLI configuration management feature to Codogotchi. It defines a configuration schema with validation rules (e.g., booleans, non-negative numbers, ISO dates, HTTPS URLs, nullable secrets), implements three CLI commands—`config get <key>`, `config set <key> <value>`, and `config list`—with type-safe parsing and atomic writes, integrates these commands into the CLI dispatcher with proper error handling and exit codes, and provides comprehensive test coverage verifying field validation, secret redaction, and error cases.

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

```

rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@docs/product/delivery/phase-01/ticket-16-cli-config.md`:
- Around line 59-60: The documentation currently contradicts itself about how
unset credentials display (the Rationale says they render as null but the
Outcome lists "<unset>"); pick one canonical representation and update both
occurrences so they match—specifically update the "Credential redaction in
`list`" sentence and the "`config get` output" lines so they both use the same
token (`null` or `"<unset>"`) consistently throughout the ticket text.
- Around line 42-61: The file contains a duplicated implementation/rationale
block describing config behavior; remove the redundant block so there is a
single authoritative description (keep the more complete version that references
codogotchiConfigSchema, SETTABLE_TOP_LEVEL/SETTABLE_HEALTH_KEYS,
resolveConfigPath, configSet and writeConfig). Edit out the earlier/shorter
duplicate paragraph and ensure the retained section includes the read-only note
for profile_id, atomic write details (writeConfig/tmp+rename), per-field
validation rules, and credential redaction/list/get behavior so all referenced
symbols (codogotchiConfigSchema, resolveConfigPath, SETTABLE_TOP_LEVEL,
configSet, writeConfig) remain documented once.

In `@packages/cli/src/config-command.ts`:
- Around line 177-183: The configGet function returns "undefined\n" for valid
but missing optional keys; update configGet (which calls getDottedValue) to
normalize undefined to a stable sentinel (e.g., null) before formatting: if the
retrieved value is undefined set it to null, then continue with the existing
string vs JSON formatting logic so callers get "null\n" (or your chosen
sentinel) instead of "undefined\n".

In `@packages/cli/src/router.ts`:
- Around line 212-216: The current "config list" branch calls configList(...)
and ignores any trailing arguments; change it to reject extra args like other
subcommands by first checking for unexpected positional args in the same way
they are validated elsewhere (i.e. ensure no trailing parameters are present for
the "list" subcommand), and if any are found return the usage/argument error and
non-zero exit code instead of proceeding to call configList; modify the block
handling sub === "list" to mirror the argument-check and error-return behavior
used by the other config subcommand branches while still calling configList({
home: getCodogotchiHome() }) when no extra args exist.
```

</details>
